mechanism_narrative: Rising monthly retail sales (RSAFS) indicate robust consumer demand, which drives corporate earnings growth and leads to positive S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC of RSAFS with 20-day forward returns is below 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window, or the signal produces negative returns in more than 50% of positive RSAFS months.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid acceleration in consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL 3-month change exceeding 0.5%) erodes real earnings and triggers monetary policy tightening, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When trigger condition is met, average 20-day forward return is not significantly negative (t-test p>0.05) or positive in more than 40% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous high unemployment (UNRATE above 6%) and declining payrolls (PAYEMS negative month-over-month) signal a weakening labor market that depresses consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the following 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal (both conditions met) fails to predict negative returns with statistical significance (p<0.05) compared to random periods over any 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: In the post-recession recovery regime, a strong rebound in industrial production (INDPRO) signals renewed economic momentum, which translates into positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as markets price in faster growth.
failure_condition: During NBER-dated expansions following recessions, the average 5-day forward return following positive INDPRO releases is not significantly positive (one-sample t-test p>0.05) or fails to beat the unconditional average.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, increases in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) compress equity valuations through higher discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In periods where the Fed is actively raising rates (FOMC hiking cycles), the rank IC of FEDFUNDS with 20-day forward returns is not negative or is above -0.02 over any 2-year span.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly increase in housing starts (HOUST rise >5%) signals a vibrant real estate market and broader economic strength, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as investors anticipate higher demand.
failure_condition: When trigger is active, average 5-day forward return is not significantly positive (p>0.05) or the signal hit rate (positive returns) is below 55% over any 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects a healthy labor market that supports consumer income and spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC of PAYEMS over 60-day forward returns is below 0.04 over any 3-year window, or the signal fails to differentiate between positive and negative return periods (Kolmogorov-Smirnov test p>0.10).

mechanism_narrative: The joint acceleration of industrial production (INDPRO) and retail sales (RSAFS) indicates synchronized supply-demand expansion, which amplifies the positive signal for S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days beyond what either series alone can provide.
failure_condition: The interaction term (INDPRO * RSAFS) in a linear model has a coefficient with p-value > 0.05 when forecasting 20-day returns, or the out-of-sample R-squared of the interaction model is not higher than the best univariate model.
